name: Adora-RGB-pick
author: dorarobotics
description: ADORA RGB camera pick-and-place skill with hand-eye calibration, black-box drop-off detection, and SPEC bridge motion control.

tools.name: get_dropoff_position
tools.description: Return the (x, y) of the target plate (drop-off zone) in meters, in the robot base frame. Read from the active robot manifest.

tools.name: pick_cube_at
tools.description: Pick up the cube at (x, y, z) meters in the robot base frame: approach from above, grasp, and lift. Caller must provide all three coordinates.

x.description: cube x in meters (SO-101 base frame)

z.description: cube z (height) in meters — typically table surface height

tools.name: set_gripper
tools.description: Open or close the gripper independently. This is a low-level utility, not the start of the yellow-block-to-black-box workflow. For that workflow, first call grasp_block, then locate_dropoff, then place_held_above/place_held_down/release_cube/retract_after_release.

action.description: open = jaws apart, close = jaws shut

width.description: Optional gripper opening width in meters. 0.0 = fully closed, 0.06 = fully open. If omitted, open→0.06, close→0.0.

tools.name: move_to
tools.description: Low-level pure Cartesian movement of the arm end-effector to (x, y, z) meters via IK. No gripper action. Do not use move_to to simulate picking or releasing a cube; use grasp_block for picking and release_cube for releasing.

x.description: target x in meters (SO-101 base frame, forward = +x)

y.description: target y in meters (left = +y, top-down view)

z.description: target z in meters (up = +z, table surface ≈ 0)

tools.name: place_cube_at
tools.description: Legacy long compound place action: carry the currently-held cube to (x, y), lower, release, retract, and return home. This can exceed hardware tool timeouts on ADORA. Prefer the split ADORA tools: place_held_above -> place_held_down -> release_cube -> retract_after_release.

tools.name: place_held_above
tools.description: Split ADORA place step 1: move the currently-held cube to a safe/home-like height above the target (x, y). Does not open the gripper. Default z is ADORA_PLACE_ABOVE_Z or the robot manifest approach_z (ADORA currently 0.138 m).

z.description: optional safe height in meters; default is ADORA_PLACE_ABOVE_Z or robot manifest approach_z

tools.name: place_held_down
tools.description: Split ADORA place step 2: lower the currently-held cube at target (x, y). Does not open the gripper. Default z is ADORA_PLACE_Z or -0.06 m.

z.description: optional lower/release height in meters; default -0.06

tools.name: release_cube
tools.description: Split ADORA place step 3: open the gripper to release the currently-held cube. Does not move the arm.

width.description: optional gripper opening width in meters; default is robot manifest open width

tools.name: retract_after_release
tools.description: Split ADORA place step 4: raise the end-effector after release at the same (x, y). Default z is ADORA_RETRACT_Z or 0.10 m.

z.description: optional retract height in meters; default 0.10

tools.name: detect_blocks
tools.description: Capture a frame from the RGB camera and detect colored blocks (red, green, blue, yellow, black) using HSV thresholding. Returns each block's color and pixel position. Optionally filter by color.

colors.description: Optional list of colors to detect. If omitted, detects all colors.

tools.name: locate_block
tools.description: Static localization only: detect a colored block and project it into the robot base frame using the current live joint state and hand-eye calibration. Does not move the arm or gripper. For the user request '抓黄色方块放到黑盒', do not start with locate_block; start with grasp_block {"color":"yellow"}.

color.description: Block color to localize. Default: yellow.

tools.name: locate_dropoff
tools.description: Detect the black drop-off box from the current camera image and return its visual geometric center in the robot base frame. Uses the center of the detected black region bounding rectangle, does not use the fixed manifest drop-off position, and does not move the arm or gripper. In the yellow-block-to-black-box workflow, call this only after grasp_block has successfully picked and lifted the yellow block. Use center_base or adjusted_base x/y for the split place tools.

tools.name: capture_chessboard
tools.description: Detect a 9x6 chessboard in the current camera frame and save the image for camera intrinsics calibration. Run this 5+ times with the chessboard at different positions/angles, then run calibrate_camera.

square_size_mm.description: Chessboard square size in millimeters. Default 25.

tools.name: calibrate_camera
tools.description: Run camera intrinsics calibration on all saved chessboard images (captured via capture_chessboard). Requires at least 5 images. Produces camera_intrinsics.json.

tools.name: hand_eye_collect
tools.description: Record one hand-eye calibration pose. Chessboard must be fixed on table. Arm must be at the desired pose (use move_to first). Reads current joint angles, captures frame, detects chessboard, saves pose pair.

tools.name: hand_eye_solve
tools.description: Solve AX=XB hand-eye calibration using collected poses. Requires 6+ poses. Saves hand_eye.json. Methods: tsai, park, horaud, andreff, daniilidis (default: park).

tools.name: auto_calibrate
tools.description: Fully automated camera intrinsics + hand-eye calibration. Place the chessboard on the table, then run this. Moves the arm through multiple poses to capture calibration data and solve AX=XB. No manual intervention needed.

tools.name: grasp_block
tools.description: Primary first tool for the request '执行Adora机械臂抓取黄色方块放到黑盒'. Autonomously pick up a colored block using hand-eye calibration: detect block via camera -> compute base-frame position -> IK solve -> close gripper -> lift. On ADORA this stops after lift with the cube held; it does not place into the black box. After success, call locate_dropoff, then place_held_above, place_held_down, release_cube, and retract_after_release.

color.description: Block color to grasp. Default: yellow.
